goal: Ship gruai as a portable, installable package that works outside the mono-repo — with verified multi-platform support, repeatable benchmark fixtures, and zero trust in prior adapter code.

risks: Multi-platform adapters (Aider, Gemini CLI) require understanding each tool's CLI interface, output format, and session model — research cost could balloon if docs are sparse or APIs are unstable.
Building a realistic fixture project (Express + React, 10-15 files, 3-4 directives, SWE-bench issues) is itself moderate-complexity work that could sprawl if not tightly scoped.
Zero-trust on prior adapters means E2E validation may uncover deep breakage in PlatformAdapter/SpawnAdapter that cascades into multi-platform work — sequencing matters.
over_engineering_flags: SWE-bench Lite integration adds complexity — limit to 1-2 pre-selected issues with known solutions, not a general SWE-bench runner.
Four platform adapters in one directive is ambitious — Aider and Gemini CLI adapters should be minimal spawn-and-parse, not full feature parity with Claude Code adapter.
challenges.recommendation: Proceed with all 7 projects. Sequence benchmark-fixture before E2E and multi-platform so they have a real test target.

projects.title: Fix path resolution for portable installation
projects.scope_summary: Fix the foundational blocker: all skills, hooks, and pipeline docs that reference cli/templates/, .claude/hooks/, or .claude/skills/ relative to CWD must resolve correctly when gruai is installed as npm package. Add hooks to package.json files array. Create .gruai/ as canonical home with symlinks to platform-specific dirs. Replace hardcoded path assumptions with package-relative resolution.

projects.title: Create npx gru-ai Interactive CLI
projects.scope_summary: Create CLI entrypoint for npx gru-ai. Add bin field to package.json. Interactive setup via readline: project name, team size (Starter/Standard/Full/Custom, min COO+CTO+1 builder), agent name customization, platform (Claude Code/Aider/Gemini CLI/Codex/Other). Scaffolds .gruai/ canonical home, symlinks platform dirs, creates .context/ tree, agent personalities, registry, CLAUDE.md. Includes 'gruai start' subcommand to launch dashboard server.

projects.title: Variable Team Size Support
projects.scope_summary: Refactor /gruai-agents skill and CLI scaffolding to support Starter (4: COO+CTO+FS+CEO), Standard (7: +CPO+FE+BE+QA), Full (11: all roles), Custom (pick from role list, minimum COO+CTO+1 builder enforced). Agent-registry.json handles variable teams. Team definitions adjust membership. Game dashboard renders partial teams gracefully.

projects.title: Ship Dashboard and Game Server in npm Package
projects.scope_summary: Ensure dist/ (game dashboard) and dist-server/ (monitoring server) are correctly built, included in package.json, and functional when installed from npm. Dashboard is NOT optional — it ships with the package. Server resolves paths relative to consumer project. 'gruai start' command launches server and serves dashboard. Verify game renders with consumer's agent-registry.json including partial teams.

projects.title: Build Repeatable Test Fixture with Predefined Directives
projects.scope_summary: Create a self-contained fixture project under tests/fixture/ containing: (1) small Express API + React frontend (10-15 files), (2) 3-4 predefined directives of varying weight — lightweight bug fix, medium feature addition, heavyweight refactor, (3) 1-2 pre-selected SWE-bench Lite issues with known solutions as additional directives, (4) validation script that checks post-run file state against expected outcomes. Each directive has directive.json, directive.md, and documented expected outcomes.

projects.title: Zero-Trust E2E Validation of Full Pipeline
projects.scope_summary: Verify the full pipeline works end-to-end from scratch, trusting NOTHING from prior work. Steps: (1) verify PlatformAdapter and ClaudeCodeAdapter actually work — if broken, fix them, (2) run npx gru-ai init against the benchmark fixture with at least 2 team configs (Starter and Standard), (3) execute each predefined directive through full pipeline (triage through completion), (4) validate outputs against expected outcomes. Explicitly test: CLI spawning, session discovery, JSONL parsing, directive lifecycle, hook execution, review gates. Fix any breakage discovered.

projects.title: Platform Adapters: Aider, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI
projects.scope_summary: Build and validate SpawnAdapter implementations for three additional platforms beyond Claude Code: (1) Aider — research CLI interface, build AiderSpawnAdapter, spawn and parse output, (2) Gemini CLI — research CLI interface, build GeminiCLISpawnAdapter, spawn and parse, (3) Codex CLI — verify existing CodexCLISpawnAdapter actually works (zero trust). Run each adapter against at least 1 fixture directive. Minimal viable adapters — spawn-and-parse, not full feature parity. Document gaps per platform.
